text:
Simone Biles has opened up on herfrustrating experience during today’s balance beam final at the Paris Olympics, describing the shushing from the crowd as ‘weird and odd’.

There were audible gasps at the Bercy Arena after an out-of-sorts Biles fell off the beam during an ambitious routine, denying the iconic American an eighth gold medal.

The 27-year-old managed to regain her composure before finishing her routine but had to settle for a fifth-placed finish, failing in her pursuit of a third gold in the French capital thissummer.

Alice D’Amato capitalised on Biles’ rare off-day to becomeItaly’s first ever Olympic champion in women’s gymnastics with a score of 14.366 and her compatriot, Manila Esposito, finished third on the leaderboard to get her hands on bronze.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris Olympics 2024

Chinese sensationZhou Yaqin, 18, competing at her first ever Olympics, scored 14.100 to take home the silver medal.

Biles never looked truly at ease and the US superstar appeared to take out her disappointment on the crowd, accusing fans inside the arena of shushing and making unnecessary noises.

Asked whether the strange atmosphere inside the Bercy Arena was the reason for her outburst, Biles told Eurosport: ‘Yes, and we’ve asked for music several times but they don’t want to play some music for some reason.

‘But I think the shushing was the loudest part. Their shushing and their trying to cheer… it was so weird and odd but it is what it is at the end of the day.

‘Beam is always a tough apparatus to do a final on and unfortunately, for a lot of us, it didn’t happen today.

‘But that’s okay because we’re still proud of whoever made the podium.’

Despite missing out on gold on the beam and on the floor, Biles said she was ‘definitely proud’ to take four medals back to the United States.

‘I’m definitely proud of the work that I’ve put in mentally and physically,’ she added.

‘Having the right group of people around me… it just goes to show that I can do it and I did it here a couple of nights in a row.

‘I’m really proud of the work and outcomes. I can’t be disappointed with any of my performances that I’ve done here at the Games.’

Biles’ success in Paris comes three years after her Tokyo ‘twisties’ which forced her to pull out of several events.

Asked if she felt like she had redeemed herself, Biles replied: ‘Most definitely.

‘I couldn’t be more proud of how I’ve done. I’m 27 years old and walking away from this Games with four medals to add to my collection. I’m not mad about it.

‘And always sharing the podium with Jordan [Chiles] is so much fun. We’ve trained together for so many years so to see her put in that hard work and for it to pay off is just amazing.

‘I think I was more excited for her than I was for myself!’

Pressed on how she will celebrate her experience at the Games, Biles responded: ‘Maybe a tattoo.

‘But most importantly I’m just going to go back to the States, see my husband and relax.’

title: Simone Biles explains why she shouted at crowd after missing out on Olympic medal | Metro News
description: Simone Biles has opened up on her experience in the balance beam final at the Olympics, describing the crowd's shushing as 'weird and odd'.
